games.description: Morphy's immortal miniature, played in a box at the opera — rapid development and a clean queen-sacrifice mate.

opening.name: Philidor Defense

games.description: Anderssen sacrifices a bishop, both rooks and the queen to deliver mate with three minor pieces.

opening.name: King's Gambit Accepted: Bishop's Gambit, Bryan Countergambit

games.description: Anderssen's 'evergreen' brilliancy, crowned by a famous queen sacrifice and a bishop-and-rook mating net.

opening.name: Italian Game: Evans Gambit, Dufresne Defense

games.description: A 13-year-old Fischer unleashes the stunning ...Be6!! queen offer and a windmill to defeat a leading American master.

opening.name: English Opening: Anglo-Indian Defense, King's Indian Formation

games.description: Kasparov's astonishing rook sacrifice on d4 launches a king hunt that drags Topalov's king from a7 to d1.

opening.name: Pirc Defense

games.event: IBM Man vs Machine, New York
games.description: The decisive Game 6: Deep Blue's knight sacrifice on e6 becomes the first match win by a computer over a world champion.

opening.name: Caro-Kann Defense: Karpov Variation, Modern Variation

games.description: Game 6 of the 1972 match — Fischer plays 1.c4 and produces a model positional game Spassky reportedly applauded.

opening.name: Queen's Gambit Declined: Tartakower Defense

games.description: Rubinstein's masterpiece ends with a cascade of sacrifices (...Rxc3, ...Rd2, ...Bxe4+, ...Rh3) that cannot be met.

opening.name: Tarrasch Defense: Symmetrical Variation

games.description: Botvinnik's celebrated 30.Ba3!! and 30...Qxa3 31.Nh5+!! deflection sequence overwhelms a legend.

opening.name: Nimzo-Indian Defense: Rubinstein System

games.description: Steinitz keeps his rook en prise for moves on end; von Bardeleben quietly left the hall rather than resign.

opening.name: Italian Game: Giuoco Piano, Greco's Attack

games.description: Short marches his king up the board (Kg1-h6!) to set up an unstoppable mate — one of the most famous king walks ever.

opening.name: Alekhine Defense: Modern Variation, Alburt Variation

games.description: Byrne resigned in a position that looked fine to spectators — only the grandmasters saw Fischer's crushing finish.

opening.name: King's Indian Defense: Fianchetto Variation, Immediate Fianchetto

games.description: Anand's ...Nde5!! and the quiet ...Be3! produce a 21st-century classic, hailed as one of the best games of the decade.

opening.name: Semi-Slav Defense: Chigorin Defense

games.description: Marshall's ...Qg3!! — placing the queen where three pieces can take it — legendarily drew a shower of gold coins.

opening.name: French Defense: Marshall Gambit

games.description: A King's Gambit fireworks display later recreated on screen in 'From Russia with Love'.

opening.name: King's Gambit Accepted: Modern Defense

games.description: Kasparov's knight lands on d3 and strangles White's position — a defining game of his rise to the title.

opening.name: Sicilian Defense: Taimanov Variation, Gary Gambit

games.event: First American Chess Congress, New York
games.description: Morphy's thunderbolt ...Qxf3!! tears open the white king and is one of the most famous combinations of the romantic era.

opening.name: Four Knights Game: Spanish Variation, Classical Variation

games.description: Reti's hypermodern strategy culminates in the elegant 24.Bf7+ and 25.Be8! winning material by force.

opening.name: English Opening: Agincourt Defense, Bogoljubow Defense

games.description: The original double-bishop sacrifice (Bxh7+ then Bxg7) — a pattern still taught to attackers over a century later.

opening.name: Bird Opening: Dutch Variation

games.description: A textbook King's Indian Attack kingside assault ending with the crisp 31.Qxh7+!! and a forced mate.

opening.name: French Defense: King's Indian Attack
